denial_letter_references.title: ProPublica — Uncovered (insurance denials reporting)
denial_letter_references.relevance: Calibrated P2 letter surface: Circular medical-necessity boilerplate.

denial_letter_text:
UNITED HEALTHCARE

Date of Notice: October 5, 2026

Dear Patient,

We have completed our review of the prior authorization request submitted by your provider on September 1, 2026, for Inpatient secondary cytoreductive surgery (Diagnosis: Ovarian cancer, platinum-sensitive recurrence, C56.9).

Based on UHC medical policy, your request is denied because Medical Necessity criteria have not been met. UHC medical policy states that a lower level of care is appropriate for this condition. Inpatient secondary cytoreductive surgery exceeds what is required. Outpatient management has not been exhausted per clinical guidelines. Therefore, the request for inpatient admission is not covered.

You or your provider may appeal this decision within 180 days.

Additional plan language regarding experimental/investigational definitions, network status, and member cost-sharing may apply and is available upon request.

Additional plan language regarding experimental/investigational definitions, network status, and member cost-sharing may apply and is available upon request.
clinical_context: The patient is a 24-year-old female with a history of platinum-sensitive recurrent ovarian cancer (C56.9). Recent laboratory and imaging studies demonstrated a significant CA-125 rise from 22 to 210 U/mL and a CT scan showing localized pelvic recurrence. The patient's primary oncologist and gynecologic oncologist determined that secondary cytoreductive surgery is medically necessary. Due to the location of the tumor recurrence, there is a high risk of bowel resection and other complex surgical interventions, necessitating an inpatient level of care for post-operative monitoring and pain management. A detailed letter of medical necessity and the supporting clinical records were submitted to the insurer requesting authorization for inpatient admission.
